title: Admin skill creator and runtime skill authoring
goal: Add a trusted meta-skill and controlled write path so administrators can create instruction-only runtime skills from a description.
requirements: Add trusted skill ai.skill_creator with guidance for creating narrow instruction-only skills.
The meta-skill must explain name, description, source_code, object_types, required_tools, trigger_examples and body structure.
The meta-skill must refuse broad, unsafe or unclear skills until the administrator narrows the scope.
Add service-layer validation for runtime SKILL.md frontmatter and body.
Add ai.skills.create_or_update or equivalent audited write-tool gated by ai.manage_skills.
Write runtime skills atomically to data/contracts/ai/skills/<skill_id>/SKILL.md using a temporary file and os.replace.
Allow only SKILL.md in MVP; reject scripts, assets and arbitrary extra files.
Validate required_tools against the tool registry.
Reset skill catalog cache after successful write if caching is implemented.
Add management commands ai_skill_list, ai_skill_validate and ai_skill_reload for operator use.
acceptance: An administrator with ai.manage_skills can create or update an instruction-only runtime skill from a description.
A user without ai.manage_skills is denied by the write-tool.
Invalid required_tools, invalid skill_id and missing description are rejected.
Runtime skill files are written only under data/contracts/ai/skills.
Skill creation writes an audit trace without storing secrets or unsafe raw content.
The new runtime skill appears in discover_skills() without process restart.
